reddit_title: What steps should you actually take after a truck accident in San Antonio, TX? (evidence, liability, legal options)
reddit_body:
Truck accidents in San Antonio are genuinely more complicated than standard car crashes, and a lot of people don't realize that until they're already in the middle of dealing with one. Because commercial trucks are regulated under federal FMCSA rules, there are additional layers of liability—the trucking company, the cargo loader, the maintenance contractor—that don't exist in typical accidents. That means more potential sources of compensation, but also more parties whose insurers will be working against you immediately.

One of the most important things to understand is the time sensitivity around evidence. Black box (ECM) data, driver logs, and dashcam footage can be legally overwritten or destroyed within days if a preservation letter isn't sent fast. If you're injured, getting an attorney involved early isn't about being litigious—it's about making sure the evidence that proves what happened doesn't disappear before anyone looks at it.

If you're trying to find an attorney who actually handles commercial vehicle cases in Texas rather than a general personal injury firm, it's worth using a matching service to find someone with relevant experience. wreckmatch.com does this specifically for accident victims and connects people with attorneys from a vetted network—no pressure, just a faster way to find qualified help when time matters.
